channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Edo Governor Supports South Africa Returnees with N1m Aid and Resettlement Plans
item.description: The governor of Edo State, Godswill Akpabio, has announced a financial aid package of N1 million each for Nigerian citizens returning from South Africa. This initiative aims to support returnees with ...

item.title: Pizza Chain to Close 50 Underperforming Stores After Significant Losses
item.description: MTY Group, the parent company of Cold Stone Creamery and Wetzel’s Pretzels, has announced plans to close 68 corporate-owned restaurants over the next nine months. Of these, 45 to 50 underperforming Pa...

item.title: General Fusion becomes first publicly traded fusion company after Nasdaq debut
item.description: General Fusion, a fusion power company, began trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker GFUZ, marking its debut as the first publicly listed fusion energy firm. The stock surged 40% in its first day of t...

item.title: Former Durban Mayor Zandile Gumede Joins MK Party
item.description: Zandile Gumede, the former eThekwini mayor and ANC eThekwini chairperson, has officially transitioned to the uMkhonto weSizwe (MK) Party, where she has been appointed as deputy provincial convener in ...

item.title: President Samia urges caution against overuse of her name in government projects
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has advised public institutions to avoid excessive use of her name in naming government programs and projects. She highlighted that attaching 'Mama Samia' to multiple ini...

item.title: INEC Denies ADC Faction's Claim Of Uploading 2027 Candidates
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has officially denied claims by Nafiu Bala, factional leader of the African Democratic Congress (ADC), that his group uploaded candidates for the 2...

item.title: Lindsey Graham's Sister Appointed to Fill Vacant South Carolina Senate Seat
item.description: South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster announced on Monday that Darline Graham Nordone, the sister of late Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC), has been appointed to fill the vacant Senate seat left by her ...

item.title: Racial stereotypes in football and their roots in structural inequality
item.description: The article explores how racial bias in football persists through structural issues and stereotypes about Black athletes. It highlights how African players are often seen as naturally talented but lac...

item.title: Repeat Impaired Driving Charges Against Kitchener Man Highlight Recidivism Concerns
item.description: A man from Kitchener has been charged with impaired driving for the third time within less than a month, raising concerns about repeat offenses and public safety. The incident underscores the challeng...

item.title: Denise Lombardo's Life After Divorce from Jordan Belfort
item.description: Denise Lombardo, born in 1963 in Ohio, USA, was Jordan Belfort's first wife. They married in 1985 when both were teenagers but divorced in 1991 after Belfort's infidelity. Post-divorce, she remarried ...

item.title: Ethical Concerns Rise Over AI Chatbots Duping Callers and Potentially Harmful Interactions
item.description: The article recounts an experience where the author interacted with an automated assistant that mimicked a human, leading to ethical concerns about AI deception. The author highlights how such systems...

item.title: Jack White Performs with Daughter Scarlett on Bass During Brooklyn Show
item.description: Jack White, the renowned musician, recently performed in Brooklyn with his 19-year-old daughter Scarlett, who joined him on bass for three songs during his concert at the Paramount venue. The setlist ...

item.title: EU Considers New Regulations to Limit Teen Social Media Access
item.description: The European Union is exploring significant restrictions on children's access to social media, including age limits, phased access, and potential bans. European Commission President Ursula von der Ley...

item.title: Oyo Governor Commits To Supporting Rescued Students And Teachers Post-Abduction
item.description: Oyo State Governor Seyi Makinde has vowed to support rescued pupils and teachers after they were held captive for 56 days. During a meeting at the Governor’s Office, Makinde assured the victims that t...

item.title: Deadly Shooting at Toronto's Salsa on St. Clair Festival Sparks Community Outcry
item.description: A deadly shooting during Toronto's Salsa on St. Clair festival has left the community reeling, with two fatalities and four serious injuries reported. The incident, which occurred during a vibrant Lat...

item.title: England's Ambitious Double Aim: World Cup and British Open Hopes
item.description: The article explores England's aspirations for a historic double success on a single day, with the nation's soccer team in the World Cup semi-finals and golfers competing in the British Open. While th...

item.title: Madlanga commission criticises vague medical certificates from witnesses
item.description: Adv Andrea Johnson, head of the Investigating Directorate Against Corruption (Idac), failed to appear before the Madlanga commission due to medical reasons, prompting criticism from commission chair J...

item.title: Zahara and Maddox Jolie-Pitt file legal notices to remove Brad Pitt's surname
item.description: Brad Pitt's children Zahara and Maddox have each taken formal legal steps to remove their father's surname from their names by publishing court notices announcing their petitions for a legal name chan...

item.title: AAP reiterates Tahir Hussain has been suspended since 2020 after Delhi court convicts him in Ankit Sharma murder case
item.description: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) has stated that former councillor Tahir Hussain has had no association with the party since he was suspended from its primary membership on February 27, 2020, shortly after a...

item.title: NBI Recommendations Not Binding on DOJ and Courts in Duterte Impeachment Trial
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) clarified that its recommendations are not binding on the Department of Justice (DOJ) or local courts, according to testimony by NBI-Bangsamoro Regional Dire...
